summary: Fixed 1 follow-up finding from Greptile's round-4 review: gt_keyword_query's ID was made scope-unique in round 2, but the stored record (and trends stale's output) still didn't carry category/property/compare_scope, so two scoped rows for the same keyword rendered identically with no way to tell which one was stale.
reason: Persisted Category/Property/CompareScope on gtKeywordQueryRecord (populated at the same trends_interest.go write site already threading these values through for gt_interest_point), and surfaced them in trends stale's output struct so distinct scoped rows for the same keyword are visibly distinguishable instead of looking like duplicates.
validated_outcome: Live dogfood full matrix: 65/65 pass. Manually confirmed live: syncing coffee under category 0 then category 71 produces two trends stale rows, now distinguishable by their category field instead of appearing as duplicates.
